reviews.review_description: For being in VR and having the ability to show the shooting in full 3d they somehow totally avoided doing this. If you want to see interviews with no new or interesting information in vr, this is for you. There was no reason for this being in VR. I felt it was a big waste of time and money. Most dissapointing quest app purchase i've made

I loved this documentary. It should be seen by the old and the young alike. Please give us more documentaries like JFK Momentum. All I can say is WOW, glad I purchased this documentary and will revisit often. It made me feel like I was there. It deserves all the accolades.

"It’s truly amazing how they combine the historical pictures so seamlessly. I especially love the D-Day experience on AVP—Targo is definitely a leader when it comes to creating such immersive experiences.

reviews.review_title: We need more documentaries like this!
reviews.review_description: Talk about putting things into perspective. This short documentary puts you into the pictures and the videos from the past giving a great perspective on the whole event. It's one thing to see it in a video, it is a whole different thing when you are now on the street or in that enclosed space seeing how it might have been to actually be there. The style in which they turn 2D into 3D is pretty interesting and the 3D interviews really help add to it. I really wish there was some more aspects to it, like actually being able to be in either position of Oswald or Kennedy to help give the perspective from either side. Glad it gave as much as it did and did not just exclude the fact that others still believe there could have been more involved, but the provided evidence has never been shown for that.

reviews.review_title: This is what VR was made for
reviews.review_description:
JFK Memento won the Auggie at the 2024 Augmented World Expo for Best Art or Film, and deservedly so - it is spectacular!!!  The concept of taking archival photographs and footage, then placing them in a 3D render of the spaces where everything happened is phenomenal, and beautifully executed here to boot.  As soon as I finished watching JFK Memento I had my husband and son watch it, and even though they're not huge VR fans, they both loved the experience.  Our dinner conversation that night was thinking of other world events we'd like to see depicted this way.  The US Capitol on January 6?  D-Day?  The tragedy on Mount Everest recounted in Jon Krakauer's book Into Thin Air?  Anything where seeing a spatial reconstruction of events deepens your understanding of how things went down would be a candidate.  (Surely there are happy events that would make good spatial storytelling, too!)  

Bravo to the TARGO team that built this - I'm eager to see everything they do in the future!  But I do have one question, which another reviewer also mentioned - why on earth is this relegated to the AppLab?  Put this baby front and center - this is how you show newbies how wonderful VR can be.

JFK Memento is a I really enjoyed the experience JFK Memento. It offers a awesome composition between visualisations of the original locations, original footage and photography and interviews with eye witnesses. They did an awesome job at compressing their video footage, therefore the app is only approx. 4 GB big. The quality of the footage is very good. The footage contains orginal radio recordings, TV recordings, newspapers or photographies all compiled into a very consistent looking product. I really saw that the makers made a lot of effort in creating an immerse and wholesome experience.

The narrator did a hell of a job and is a really good voice actor. I am really a big fan of (flat) documentaries. There are too less good VR-documentaries at the moment but JFK Memento is definately one of them. The only downside is that it is only approx. 35 minutes long. For fans of history documentaries I definately recommend this app.

reviews.review_title: Incredible. One of the best uses of VR!
reviews.review_description:
I’m a Kennedy fan and snatched this up as soon as I heard about it. It’s really moving to actually see these locations and hear from eye witnesses “in person”. This gives you a whole new perspective on what happened that day. If you’ve seen the World Trade Center experience, this is very much in that vein.

I’ll be showing this to everyone who gets their hands on my headset!

Just finished watching the documentary, I can’t believe how much it took me there. The transitions and overlaying of actual footage in 3d with actual witnesses describing everything really put me there.
And for someone who grew up on the other side of the world and had only little knowledge of the event. 
I felt like I was there and have a better insight without the hype of conspiracies.
Well done.

Just finished this experience - And it is an "experience" in the clever way they have incorporated archival footage into a virtual render of key locations.  You almost feel like you are there to witness the tragic events unfold.   This is THE way to watch documentaries from now on.  You get a perfect sense of how it all happened, the locations it happened in and the scale of everything as well.  I only wish it had of been longer. 
This kind of content is going to revolutionize learning in the classroom.  Well done to everyone in the TARGO team.

This is the future of documentaries. We are transported in history in a totally unique way. TARGO team is brillant. I enjoyed every minute and want more experiences like that. It definitely worth the money.  I highly recommend it to anyone.

This 6DoF documentary was quite interesting from a thematic perspective as well as technological. They do a great job at taking old black and white photographs and projecting them into a 3D scene. It’s a mix of many techniques and it comes together quite well. 

Really well done, also a big kudos for adding chapters to allow for replay-ability without needing to start over from the start.

reviews.review_title: A must have for history or JFK buffs
reviews.review_description:
Or anyone that loves a well presented documentary! I love how the information was provided. Even with most of the information already being known to me, having it presented in this way ads so much to the experience, understanding and "weight" OF the information being provided.

I recommend it highly!

Targo have produced an absolute masterpiece here. This experience really makes you feel like you're there, standing in the real places that were so vital during the JFK assassination. They have blended multiple approaches to really bring out the best in VR and have somehow managed to bring historical footage to life like never before. Narrative driven, immersive experiences like this one really are the future of storytelling, and this is an absolute must experience for anyone with even a small amount of interest in historical events like this.

This app is a great example of immersive documentary storytelling. An impressive blend of archival footage with game engine environments really takes you to this momentous time and place. Should be on the main store (not just AppLab) so more people can find it.
Living history and being in History!
I've never seen something like that <3

Step into the past with 'JFK Momento' and prepare to be captivated by an immersive virtual reality experience like no other. This 35-minute documentary takes you back to the fateful year of 1963, allowing you to witness the events that shook the world and forever changed history.

One of the standout features of 'JFK Momento' is its incredible use of 3D video and photo content. Through the magic of virtual reality, iconic archival footage and photographs are brought to life in stunning stereoscopic detail. You'll feel as though you're truly standing in the midst of history, surrounded by the sights and sounds of the time.

What sets this documentary apart is its commitment to capturing historical witness testimony. As you navigate through the five chapters, you'll hear from the last living witnesses, journalists, and investigators who were directly involved in the case. Their accounts provide a unique and historically accurate perspective on the assassination and its subsequent investigation.

The graphic creativity and seamless scene transitions in 'JFK Momento' are truly impressive. The documentary takes full advantage of the virtual reality medium, using it to create visually stunning and engaging sequences. The integration of videography and photography seamlessly blends together, enhancing the overall storytelling experience.

Narration plays a crucial role in any documentary, and 'JFK Momento' excels in this aspect. The clear and articulate narration guides you through the narrative with great flow, ensuring that you never miss a beat. The combination of compelling storytelling and immersive visuals keeps you fully engaged from start to finish.

Produced by the Emmy®-nominated immersive studio TARGO, in collaboration with the Sixth Floor Museum at Dealey Plaza in Dallas and with the support of Meta, 'JFK Momento' is a groundbreaking VR documentary that offers an extraordinary understanding of a pivotal moment in history. Prepare to be transported back in time and experience history like never before.

This needs to be in the main Meta Quest store rather than App Lab!
Such a smart way to present this story and the execution is also brilliant. Don't miss this.

We’ve all probably heard about JFK’s assassination, seen bits of archive footages in various documentaries here and there, but this is…different! They put you right there on the sidewalk as the Kennedys motorcade passes by, at the vantage point of the shooter in a surprising mix of re-situated footages and pictures through a recreated Dallas. It is truly immersive and tense.
As we follow the events before and after the assassination through the eyes of multiple viewpoints (a journalist, an onlooker at the motorcade, a police investigator and more) I got a spatial and temporal understanding of that event that was captivating and chilling.
They need to make more of these !

All my life i was attracted to stereoscopic experiences. They have something magical. 
This documentary belongs to one of the best. 
Accurate realtime 3d models, incredible 3d conversions packed in a engaging documentary.
Congratulations to all the people who worked on this. Incredible work. 
Highly recommended. 
Best regards, throughview.eu
